disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid expansion of thick scaly plaques across trunk and extensor surfaces over 48 hours in a patient with long-standing psoriasis
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me what the skin looks like and how it has changed?
How quickly have the patches spread?
Where on your body is it affecting you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The scaly patches have spread really fast over the last two days — across my chest and back and on my elbows and knees. They're thicker than usual and much angrier.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Plaques are actively fissuring and bleeding; serosanguinous exudate staining bed linen — a breach in the skin barrier
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin cracking or bleeding at all?
Are any of the patches open or weeping?
What happens to the patches when you move?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, some of them have split right open and they're bleeding — the sheets have gone pink and weepy where the cracks are.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound immobilising systemic fatigue described as a full-body battery drain, accompanying the flare
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting you day to day?
You mentioned exhaustion — can you describe it?
Are you able to get up and look after yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm completely drained — like my whole battery has gone flat. I can't get up, I can't get dressed, my partner is doing everything for me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Ran out of / stopped high-potency topical corticosteroid three days ago — abrupt cessation and undertreatment preceding the flare
trigger_question_examples: Are you still using your steroid cream, and when did you last have any?
Have you run out of any of your usual skin treatments?
Has anything changed with your creams recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I ran out of my strong steroid cream about three days ago and haven't been able to get more — that's about when it really took off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Afebrile — home-measured temperature 37.4 C, no fever or rigors
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to take your temperature — any fever?
Have you had any fevers, shivering or shakes?
Do you feel hot and cold or have the sweats and chills?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I checked with the thermometer at home and it was 37.4 — I don't feel feverish and I haven't had any shivering or shakes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visible sterile pustules, small pus-filled blisters, or lakes of pus (screening for generalized pustular psoriasis)
trigger_question_examples: Are there any small pus-filled blisters or pimple-like spots appearing in the redness?
Have you noticed any crops of little pustules or pus in the patches?
Is any of it turning into small blisters filled with fluid or pus?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no pus-filled blisters or little bumps like that — it's just the thick cracked scaly patches.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Redness is widespread and bilateral/symmetric rather than one unilateral hot, rapidly expanding area (screening for cellulitis)
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness on both sides of your body, or is there one hot spreading patch?
Is any single area much hotter, more painful or spreading faster than the rest?
Does one spot feel hot and swollen compared with the others?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on both sides really — chest, back, both elbows and knees. There's no single hot spot that's worse or spreading faster than the rest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Flare preceded by a period of high psychosocial stress — a recognised psoriasis trigger
trigger_question_examples: Has anything been going on in your life lately — stress or big changes?
Do you know of anything that might have set this flare off?
How have things been for you recently, generally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I've been under a huge amount of stress lately — that's around when the skin started getting worse.
